fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kentucky Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kentucky - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity kentucky, how to transfer pharmacist license to kentucky, pharmacist compact kentucky, kentucky pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Kentucky Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 125 W. Main St., Suite 210, Frankfort, KY 40601

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Kentucky.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Navigate to the KBN/KBP Licensure Portal, create a new user account if you don't have one, and complete the 'Pharmacist Licensure' application, selecting either 'By Examination' or 'By Endorsement'.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your ACPE-accredited pharmacy school to send your official transcripts directly to the Kentucky Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Register for and complete the required criminal background check as directed by the Board (typically involves fingerprinting through a specified vendor).

steps.title: Pass the MPJE
steps.description: Register with NABP (National Association of Boards of Pharmacy) to take and pass the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) for Kentucky.

steps.title: Pass the NAPLEX (By Examination Applicants)
steps.description: If applying for initial licensure by examination, register with NABP to take and pass the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX).

steps.title: Complete NABP Licensure Transfer (Endorsement Applicants)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, complete the NABP Pharmacist License Transfer Application and ensure all necessary verifications from prior states of licensure are sent to the Kentucky Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Kentucky pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Kentucky have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Kentucky. To work in Kentucky, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Kentucky?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kentucky while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Kentucky
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Kentucky, use the KBN/KBP Licensure Portal, the online system provided by the Kentucky Board of Pharmacy for all applications.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the KBN/KBP Licensure Portal. Click on 'New User Registration'. Enter your Social Security Number, First and Last Name, and Date of Birth to create an account. Once registered, log in and select the appropriate application type for 'Pharmacist Licensure'.
